trivia_bullets.text: Jeskai watermark rare from Khans of Tarkir (2014-09-26). Type line is Creature — Djinn Wizard. Djinn are the Jeskai Way's resident blue race in the KTK block, associated with wisdom and aerial dominance. KTK contains five Djinn creatures total, all Jeskai-aligned.
sources: Gatherer: KTK Djinn creature type listing

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: 'No one petal claims beauty for the lotus.' Speaker is not attributed on the card. The lotus image appears directly in the card art (the figure holds a glowing lotus) and encodes the Jeskai teaching of collective humility — individual skill is subsumed into the whole.
Illustrated by Chase Stone. Card received a promotional foil printing as the rare in the Khans of Tarkir 'Jeskai Monks' intro pack (Scryfall set code PKTK, card 195).
sources: Cardkingdom: Intro Pack Rare Foil listing

trivia_bullets.text: Flying 3/4 for 5 mana; lifelink trigger fires on any noncreature spell. EDHREC records 1,918 Commander decks (0.26% of all tracked decks), with 15% inclusion in Jeskai lifegain builds and a +14% synergy score. Meaningful utility in spell-heavy Jeskai builds but not a broad staple.

Vision filter tag `creature-sphinx` is incorrect — Scryfall type_line confirms Djinn Wizard, not Sphinx. Djinn and Sphinx have distinct MTG visual identities (Sphinx typically depicts lion-body or hawk-head iconography). Flag for janitor correction.

flags.vision_filter_error: tags_filter contains `creature-sphinx` but Scryfall type_line is Creature — Djinn Wizard. Needs janitor correction. The featureless mask and levitating robed figure are consistent with MTG's Djinn visual language.
